summary: Cleaned up the debug/development module db_2253 by replacing many address-based function and data names with behavior-based names, adding typed structures for button state and debug UI state, marking internal helpers/data static, and propagating newly understood symbol names across config, headers, and call sites. The PR also completed or improved several db_2253 decomp fragments, most notably fn_SetupSoundInfo reaching 100% per the decomp.dev bot report, while overall matched code stayed effectively flat at 26.76% with +132 bytes.
what_changed: Renamed db_2253 exported/debug functions from address names to descriptive names such as db_GetGameLaunchButtonState, db_Setup, db_ButtonsDown, db_ButtonsPressed, db_ButtonsRepeat, db_PrintEntityCounts, db_PrintThreadInfo, db_RunEveryFrame, db_HandleItemPokemonMenuInput, db_CheckAndSpawnItem, db_InitScreenshot, db_CheckScreenshot, db_TakeScreenshotIfPending, db_ClearFPUExceptions, and db_SetupCrashHandler.

Renamed many internal db_2253 helpers around debug menu features: item and Pokemon menu setup/update/check, CPU handicap info, animation info, camera info, sound info, miscellaneous fighter/stage/visual effects, 5x speed, bonus info, and object allocation limiter.

Renamed global and static data from lbl/db address labels into typed debug-state names, including db_ButtonStates, db_ItemAndPokemonMenuText_buf, db_ItemAndPokemonMenu, db_CpuHandicapInfo, db_AnimationInfo, db_CameraInfoDisplay_buf, db_SoundInfoText_buf, db_gameLaunchButtonState, db_SoundToggles, db_ShowSoundInfo, db_MiscStageVisualsStatus, db_MiscVisualEffectsStatus, db_5xSpeedStatus, db_ScreenshotPending, and db_ScreenshotNumber.

Introduced field names for previously anonymous debug structs, especially button state fields current/prev/pressed/released/repeat and item/Pokemon menu fields like DisplayStatus, DisplayFadeTimer, ItemSpawnsEnabled, Player, CurrentlySelectedItem, CurrentlySelectedPokemon, LastSelectedItem, and LastSelectedPokemon.

Replaced raw controller bitmasks in db_2253 with HSD_PAD constants such as HSD_PAD_B, HSD_PAD_X, HSD_PAD_Y, HSD_PAD_R, HSD_PAD_L, HSD_PAD_DPADUP, HSD_PAD_DPADDOWN, HSD_PAD_DPADLEFT, and HSD_PAD_DPADRIGHT.
Added small inline wrappers DevText_SetBGColor2 and DevText_SetTextColor2 taking GXColor by value and forwarding to the pointer-taking DevText APIs, used in debug text setup code.
Marked many formerly global db_2253 helpers and data as static in the C/static header context, reducing implied external surface where functions are only module-internal.

Renamed cross-module symbols and propagated call sites: lbArchive_80016C64 to lbArchive_LoadSymbols, Camera_80030740 to Camera_SetBackgroundColor, Camera_80030AA4 to Camera_SetStageVisible, ftLib_800860E8/80086140 to ftLib_IsMasterHandPresent/ftLib_IsCrazyHandPresent, gm_8016B274/8016B2C8 to gm_SetGameSpeed/gm_ResetGameSpeed, Ground_801C1FFC to Ground_ApplyStageBackgroundColor, and ifAll_802F3698/802F36A4/802F36B0 to ifAll_HideHUD/ifAll_ShowHUD/ifAll_IsHUDHidden.
Renamed mushroom-related fighter functions in symbols/call sites to Fighter_SuperMushroomApply, Fighter_SuperMushroomEnd, Fighter_PoisonMushroomApply, and Fighter_PoisonMushroomEnd.

Moved or exposed needed extern declarations through more appropriate headers, including efLib_80458EB0 in eflib.h, HSD object allocation related declarations in baselib/particle.h and psappsrt.h, lbShadow_8000EEE0 in lbshadow.h, and lbAudioAx declarations used by the debug sound info code.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt to reflect new symbol names for db_2253 and related modules.
smart_moves: The cleanup did not just rename functions; it introduced structures and field names that encode behavior. db_ButtonStates with current/prev/pressed/released/repeat makes the frame input logic much easier to audit than x0/x4/x8/xC/x10.
Replacing numeric pad masks with HSD_PAD constants documents the debug-menu chord logic directly and makes future matching changes less error-prone.
The PR propagated names through symbols.txt, scratches.txt, headers, and cross-module call sites in one pass, avoiding a split state where C names and symbol config disagree.
Static-scope tightening separated internal debug helpers from true exported entry points, which helps future reviewers distinguish module-private matching scaffolding from API surface.
The static header retained address comments beside renamed symbols and data, preserving searchability by address while improving semantic readability.
The inline DevText color wrappers avoided duplicating pointer-temporary boilerplate at call sites and likely helped preserve matching-sensitive call/stack behavior while making the code clearer.
The sound debug section moved from undecompiled stubs to named setup/update/check routines and reached 100% for fn_SetupSoundInfo according to the bot report.
decomp_lessons: When a large debug module has many address-named helpers, first recover feature boundaries from repeated setup/update/check patterns, then name functions as fn_SetupX, fn_UpdateX, and fn_CheckX if they remain internal.

Use typed per-feature state structs to replace anonymous global blobs. Even partial names like DisplayStatus or CurrentlySelectedPokemon immediately clarify control flow and can improve future matching work.
For input-heavy debug code, define helper accessors such as db_ButtonsDown, db_ButtonsPressed, and db_ButtonsRepeat and express conditions with named pad constants rather than raw bitmasks.
Keep address comments on prototypes and static data during cleanup; they preserve decomp searchability while allowing semantic names.
Renaming a callee such as lbArchive_LoadSymbols or Camera_SetBackgroundColor should be propagated to config symbols and all call sites in the same PR to avoid symbol drift.
Expose externs in the owning subsystem headers instead of keeping local ad hoc extern declarations in a debug module when the declarations are broadly useful or semantically belong elsewhere.
Bot match deltas can be mixed for a cleanup PR: some functions regress in matched bytes while others improve, but the net result can still be useful if names, structs, and headers unlock future work.
naming_conventions: Use db_ prefix for exported debug-module functions and state: db_Setup, db_RunEveryFrame, db_ButtonStates, db_ScreenshotPending.
Use fn_ prefix for static/internal helpers when no subsystem-exported name is intended, but still make the suffix semantic: fn_SetupSoundInfo, fn_CheckCameraInfo, fn_Toggle5xSpeed.
Use Setup/Update/Check naming for recurring debug UI lifecycles.
Use Is/Are for predicate functions: ftLib_IsMasterHandPresent, ftLib_IsCrazyHandPresent, db_AreItemSpawnsEnabled, ifAll_IsHUDHidden.
Use Enable/Disable pairs for toggled debug features: db_EnableItemSpawns/db_DisableItemSpawns and fn_EnableShowItemPickupRange/fn_DisableShowItemPickupRange.
Use Apply/End for status-effect lifecycle functions: Fighter_SuperMushroomApply, Fighter_SuperMushroomEnd, Fighter_PoisonMushroomApply, Fighter_PoisonMushroomEnd.
Use Set/Reset for game-mode state mutations: gm_SetGameSpeed and gm_ResetGameSpeed.
Use subsystem prefixes matching existing project style: Camera_, Ground_, Fighter_, ftLib_, gm_, lbArchive_, ifAll_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted anonymous globals into static structs with explicit field order and address comments, preserving layout while improving source readability.
Used bitfields for compact debug flags such as ShowEnemyStompRange, ShowItemPickupRange, ShowCoinPickupRange, ShowFighterCollisionBubbles, MiscFighterVisualsStatus, and ShowAnimationInfo.
Replaced raw bit tests with named HSD_PAD constants without changing the underlying control-flow intent.
Added static inline wrappers for DevText_SetBGColor and DevText_SetTextColor so callers can pass GXColor literals/locals while preserving a pointer-based API call.
Marked local helpers static, which can matter for matching and symbol visibility while documenting that the functions are not external APIs.

The bot report shows matching impact was carefully tracked: fn_SetupSoundInfo reached 100.00%, fn_UpdateSoundInfo improved to 94.29%, fn_CheckSoundInfo improved to 56.34%, while some renamed/refactored functions had small negative byte deltas.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only PR comment was the decomp.dev bot report for GALE01, reporting matched code at 26.76% with +132 bytes overall and per-function deltas in db_2253 and it_266F.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for db_804D6B48 or db_804D6B98 to see whether the remaining partially named debug state structs were fully identified.
Search for fn_80225A54, fn_802277E8, fn_802279E8, fn_80227BA8, fn_80227D38, fn_80227EB0, fn_80228124, fn_80228D38, and fn_8022900C to find follow-up decomp/naming work in db_2253.
Check whether db_2253.h later moved static/internal prototypes out of the public header or otherwise cleaned up header layering.
Search for lbAudioAx_80028B2C, lbAudioAx_80028B4C, and lbAudioAx_80025064 to continue naming audio debug counters and toggles.
Search for db_SoundRelated_6B68, db_SoundRelated_6B6C, db_SoundRelated_6B70, and db_SoundRelated_6B74 for later recovery of sound-info state semantics.
